note: Chinese sim racing manufacturer est. 2020. PC-only ecosystem. Partners with McLaren Customer Racing and ASUS ROG. RaceSims is authorized Indian distributor.

conspit_link.name: Conspit Link 2.0
features: Device detection and management for all Conspit peripherals
Real-time temperature monitoring (Phase U/V/W, MOS temps)
LED color and telemetry-linked behavior control

modes.advanced: Comprehensive filtering, parameter customization, torque boost activation

conspit_link.pro_profiles: Laurin Heinrich (pro driver) profiles for AC and iRacing
known_limitations: Advanced wheelbase settings somewhat simplified compared to competitors
Pushes users toward presets rather than deep control
Paddle calibration may need recalibration after reboot
SimHub compatibility varies by wheel model

simhub_compatibility.310_APEX: SimHub RGB LED control only, NOT custom dashboards
simhub_compatibility.290GP: Conspit proprietary dash only, NO SimHub

led_status_indicators.green_slow_flash: Wheelbase axis has exceeded limit — recalibrate steering angle
led_status_indicators.blue_slow_flash: Motor error — check Conspit Link software for diagnostic info

troubleshooting.issue: Force feedback feels inconsistent or jerky
solutions: Update to latest firmware via Conspit Link 2.0 — Conspit has significantly improved FFB through firmware updates
Try different pre-configured sim profiles in Conspit Link (game-specific presets available)
Switch to Advanced Mode and adjust filtering parameters
Reduce overall FFB strength to stay within the base's clean operating range
For Ares 12: activate 12Nm boost mode in Advanced settings if running at default 10Nm
Check if you're using the right sim profile — each game has different FFB characteristics
Connect USB-C directly to PC mainboard — avoid USB hubs
Try different USB ports (USB 3.0 recommended)
Check that the power supply is connected (48V for Ares 12/Platinum, 24V for Apex)
Reinstall Conspit Link 2.0 from conspit.com/download
Check Device Manager for unknown devices and update drivers
Try a different USB-C cable
troubleshooting.escalation: If base is detected but shows motor errors (blue LED flash), contact RaceSims support

troubleshooting.issue: Quick release has play or wobble
solutions: For CDR: ensure pull ring is fully engaged until you hear/feel a click
For CDP: verify the engagement ring is fully locked
Some early CDP/CDR units had manufacturing variance in tolerance
Tighten the QR mounting screws (6x M6 on CDP)
If wobble persists after checking engagement, contact RaceSims — Conspit has been responsive to replacement requests

troubleshooting.issue: Phantom brake input / brake always slightly engaged
symptoms: Car brakes slightly without pressing pedal
Brake axis not at zero at rest
solutions: The hydraulic pressure sensor is extremely sensitive — this is normal behavior that needs calibration
Set a small dead zone in Conspit Link 2.0 after calibration (critical step)
Recalibrate the brake axis: press firmly for max, release completely for min
Don't rest your foot on the brake pedal — even very light pressure registers
Check in-game dead zone settings as well

troubleshooting.issue: Brake feel changes over time or feels soft
symptoms: Brake feels softer than when new
solutions: Increase preload using the adjustment knob and included tool — this is expected maintenance
The warning label on CPP EVO pedals instructs users to increase preload after use — this is normal
Do NOT increase hydraulic preload excessively — gradual adjustment only
For CPP EVO: use the 4-level pedal force adjustment to find your preferred setting
Check that all mounting bolts are tight — flex in the rig can affect brake feel

troubleshooting.issue: Clutch feels rough or grainy (CPP EVO)
solutions: This is a known break-in issue — smoothness improves significantly with use
Material wearing from the sleeve is normal during the initial break-in period
Keep using the clutch normally — it will smooth out over 20-50 uses
If roughness persists after extended use, contact RaceSims support

symptoms: Pedals not appearing in Conspit Link
solutions: Pedals can connect via USB-C directly to PC OR via CAN port through Conspit wheelbase — try both methods
If using the vibration module (M-DVF), you MUST use direct PC USB connection
Try a different USB port — avoid USB hubs
Make sure USB-C and power cables are connected to the controller box BEFORE mounting to rig
Reinstall Conspit Link 2.0

symptoms: Have to recalibrate paddles every restart
solutions: This is a known software issue in some firmware versions
After calibrating: click MIN when released, MAX when fully pressed
ALWAYS click 'Save to Flash' after calibration — this persists through reboots
Update to latest firmware via Conspit Link — this issue has been improved in recent updates
If it persists, save your profile and reload it after reboot as a workaround

troubleshooting.issue: Display not showing custom SimHub dashboards
symptoms: Wheel screen shows default or no dashboard
solutions: Check SimHub compatibility for your specific wheel model:
MAX 01 = Full SimHub dashboard support
310 APEX = SimHub RGB LED control only, NOT custom dashboards
290GP = Conspit proprietary dash only, NO SimHub at all
CSD Dashboard = Full SimHub with presets
For MAX 01: install SimHub, select dashboard preset or create custom
Make sure SimHub is running and the correct device plugin is enabled
In Conspit Link: use rotary switch as modifier key first, then configure encoder functions
Save to flash after making any changes
Check if you're in Basic or Advanced mode — some remapping options only available in Advanced
Try closing and reopening Conspit Link after saving

troubleshooting.issue: Brake pedal tray flex under heavy braking
symptoms: Pedal plate flexes when braking hard
Soft feel from rig rather than pedal
solutions: More noticeable in firmer brake configurations on CPP EVO V2
Ensure pedal baseplate is rigidly mounted to rig with all bolts tightened
Use firmer rig mounting points — this is usually a rig issue, not a pedal issue
Consider adding reinforcement to the pedal deck area of your rig
The hydraulic system can generate significant force — budget rigs may flex
Conspit bases have no active cooling — they rely on passive cooling
No thermal throttling has been observed in independent testing
Monitor temperatures via Conspit Link 2.0 real-time readout (Phase U/V/W, MOS temps)
Normal operating temperatures vary by load — sustained high-torque use will generate more heat
Ensure adequate airflow around the base — don't box it in

ares_20_platinum.name: ARES 20 Platinum
ares_20_platinum.motor: Customized skew-pole direct-drive with anti-cogging (Dual Skewed Pole, Asymmetric Slot-to-Pole)
ares_20_platinum.mounting: Bottom mount (M6, 80mm, Fanatec-pattern compatible) + Side mount (M8)
ares_20_platinum.connectivity: USB-C to mainboard, 5x USB-C ecosystem ports, E-Stop port, 6-pin magnetic connector
ares_20_platinum.qr_options: CDP (7075 aluminum with pogo pins) or CDR

ares_12.name: ARES 12
ares_12.note: Same architecture as Ares 20 Platinum. Boost mode unlocks full 12Nm in Advanced settings.

ares_apex.name: ARES APEX
ares_apex.encoder: 21-bit magnetic encoder (lower resolution than optical on higher models)

cpp_evo_v2.name: CPP EVO V2
cpp_evo_v2.brake: Gen 2.0 Dual-Stage Hydraulic with 20 MPa pressure sensor
cpp_evo_v2.haptics: M-DVF vibration motors on each pedal (SimHub + iRacing compatible)
cpp_evo_v2.materials: CNC-machined T6 aluminum alloy, sandblasted and anodized
cpp_evo_v2.adjustability: 4-level pedal force, independent preload for both brake stages, 4-level clutch bite point, angle and height adjustable
cpp_evo_v2.connection: USB-C direct or CAN port via Conspit wheelbase

cpp_lite.name: CPP Lite
cpp_lite.brake: Hydraulic system (approx half braking force of EVO)

cpp_apex.name: CPP APEX
cpp_apex.note: Optional M-DVF Vibration Module available (requires direct PC connection)

max_01_wheel.name: MAX 01
max_01_wheel.controls: 10 RGB buttons + 2 rear, 4 rotary encoders, 4 thumb encoders, 2 funky switches (7-way)
max_01_wheel.paddles: 2 magnetic Hall shift + 2 analog Hall clutch

290gp_wheel.name: 290 GP
290gp_wheel.controls: 12 RGB buttons, 4 rotary encoders, 3 rotary switches, 2 seven-way joysticks
290gp_wheel.note: Conspit proprietary dash only, no SimHub

300gt_wheel.name: 300 GT
300gt_wheel.materials: T-300 carbon fiber prepreg front + aluminum unibody back
300gt_wheel.controls: 8 RGB buttons + 2 rear, 3 rotary switches, 4 thumb encoders, 2 seven-way joysticks

310_apex_wheel.name: 310 APEX
310_apex_wheel.materials: Full aluminum alloy + steel, microfiber rim
310_apex_wheel.controls: 8 RGB buttons, 2 thumb encoders, 2 twelve-position rotary switches, 2 seven-way joysticks
310_apex_wheel.paddles: 4 aluminum paddles (2 digital shift + 2 analog clutch, all Hall sensored)
310_apex_wheel.compatibility: CDR or CDP QR, compatible with MOZA, Simagic, PXN bases via CDR

h3_handbrake.name: H3 Handbrake
h3_handbrake.features: Adjustable preload, 4 force levels, switchable horizontal/vertical lever
h3_handbrake.materials: CNC-machined aluminum, sealed bearing system, zero mechanical play

csd_dashboard.name: CSD Dashboard
csd_dashboard.software: Full SimHub integration with ready-to-use presets

apex_bundle.name: Apex Bundle
apex_bundle.contents: Ares Apex 8Nm + 310 Apex Wheel + CPP Apex Pedals

warranty.duration: Manufacturer warranty generally 2-3 years (varies by retailer)
warranty.process_racesims: Contact RaceSims support first. We handle all Conspit warranty claims for Indian customers. Remote troubleshooting first, then replacement coordination with Conspit.
warranty.support_primary: Conspit Discord server — manufacturer technicians actively respond

pedals: Open Conspit Link 2.0, detect your pedals
Calibrate each axis: press firmly for max, release completely for min
Set a small dead zone for brake (CRITICAL — hydraulic sensor is extremely sensitive)
Configure haptic/vibration settings if using M-DVF motors
Save to Flash to persist through reboots
clutch_paddles: In Conspit Link: click MIN when paddle is released
Click MAX when paddle is fully pressed
Select clutch mode: Dual Axis, Manual, or Set Bite Point
For bite point: drag purple slider to desired engagement point
NOTE: May need recalibration after reboot on some firmware versions
wheelbase: Connect USB-C directly to PC mainboard
Connect appropriate power supply (48V for Ares 12/Platinum, 24V for Apex)
Calibrate steering angle — set maximum desired rotation
Select or customize a sim profile
